paragraphs.body: An unknown source once said, “It’s not what I have been through in my life that defines who I am.. It’s how I got through it that makes me the person I am today.” I always thought my parents would be the underlying source of my stability. They are the ones who provided me with a roof to live over my head, clothes to put on my body, food to keep me alive, a bed to sleep in at night, and so on. They were the ones there during the ups and downs in my life and offered me emotional support through it all. But they were not the true source of my stability that helped me get through the hard times. I never thought a simple stuffed animal that was given to me would be the one thing that would bring comfort and calmness during the hard times of my life. That stuffed animal happens to be a big stuffed cat that I named Big Kitty.

annotations.comment: Interesting intro! Nicely written with a lovely little surprise at the end :)
annotations.context_left: night, and so on. They were the ones there during the ups and downs in my life and offered me

paragraphs.body: Growing up, Big Kitty was my favorite stuffed animal that I had. Big Kitty was my aunt’s stuffed animal before she was mine, and when my aunt was grown up she decided to pass her down to me. When I first got her, she had brown and black spots on her back and around her eyes, snowy white fur, very fluffy and soft to the touch, she had these big bright blue eyes, and she smelled like she just came fresh out of the washer. Throughout the years as I got older, Big Kitty had definitely changed her appearance, smell, and softness. Her snowy white fur was no longer that bright shade of white, rather it started to turn a cream color then into a light brown where it has presently stayed like that. She had no longer smelled like the fresh laundry scent when I first got her, instead she would sometime smell like warm sugar vanilla or coconut based on the perfume I was wearing. Her fur was no longer fluffy and soft, but rather feeling like an old rough carpet. One of her big bright blue eyes got smashed into the cement tiles by one of my siblings. At first, we tried to super glue it back onto her, but the glue was too sticky and covered her whole eye making it cloudy, not showing her bright blue eyes. Soon the glue wore off and the eye popped off leaving her with this cracked bright blue surface for an eye. Even though Big Kitty’s appearance, smell, and softness changed from when I first got her, I never thought of getting rid of her. Her smell and texture of fur made me feel safe and secure when things in my life were going well. Her appearance in the distance or close-up gave me a sense of stability that she was always going to be there for me no matter what.

annotations.text: At first, we tried to super glue it back onto her, but the glue was too sticky and covered her whole eye making it cloudy, not showing her bright blue eyes. Soon the glue wore off and the eye popped off leaving her with this cracked bright blue surface for an eye.
annotations.comment: I'm interested in the way your descriptions show a progression of wear and tear, deterioration, etc...I'm interested to see if this is a metaphor that will play in other ways in this essay?

paragraphs.body: February 19, 2017, should have been a day of celebration for my sister’s thirteenth birthday, but it ended in mourning of the stable life I once had. It was an hour before the guests were supposed to arrive for my sister’s birthday party. I heard screaming and crying from the living room downstairs. Immediately, I rushed downstairs to see what was happening, and I saw my mom looking at the computer screen in tears. The computer was open to my stepdad’s Facebook Messenger, where I saw intimate messages from him and another woman, who was not my mother. Instantly, tears came out of my eyes, and I immediately had to console my heartbroken mother. After each of our emotions calmed down, my mom called my aunt to come over to help her deal with the situation and the number of girls that were supposed to arrive soon. In that moment none of us knew what the future would look like for us, and for me, that was a scary feeling. So, I decided to go up to my bedroom to have a moment to myself because at that moment I felt like my life was falling out of control. The first thing that I looked for was Big Kitty. As soon as my eye caught her sitting on my bed, I ran over to her, picked her up, and squeezed her as tight as I could. Her fairly soft fur, vanilla scent, and presence brought such security and stability that I needed in that moment of chaos. For the rest of the night, I carried Big Kitty around to give me that stability to be able to get through the remainder of the night and put on a happy face for my sister, mom, aunt, and guests. When I went to bed after all the chaos that happened that night, I hugged Big Kitty so tight until I was finally able to fall asleep and able to forget how my life was now not in control.

paragraphs.body: A stuffed animal is more than just a toy; they become your friends and confidants. They create imaginative play, allowing children to make stories and scenarios. Stuffed animals help develop social skills when children play with them. Stuffed animals can instill a sense of responsibility in children. They can evoke memories and serve as cherished keepsakes throughout life. But, to me, a stuffed animal holds so much more meaning than these things. A stuffed animal is like a blanket of stability that provides a sense of calmness through the chaos that life throws at us. Big Kitty provided comfort and security to me during all the stressful times in my life. She was there during the time of my own parents' divorce. She was there for me through all the fights between my mom, stepsister, stepbrother, and stepdad. She was there the night I found out that my stepdad had been cheating on my mom with another woman. She was present throughout my whole battle with depression, anxiety, and anorexia. She was there when I decided to move to a new school during my junior year of high school. A stuffed animal represents the one person who can never leave your side during the ups and downs in your life. Big Kitty pushes me to continue living my life, striving towards my future goals, even when life likes to throw curve balls in my direction.

annotations.text: stuffed animal represents the one person who can never leave your side during the ups and downs in your life. Big Kitty pushes me to continue living my life, striving towards my future goals, even when life likes to throw curve balls in my direction.
annotations.comment: The metaphor emerges quite strongly, here. Neat.

paragraphs.body: Now that I am in college, I have had a lot of big changes that have occurred. I no longer have my parents’ help guide me in the right direction, I have had to become more independent in making my own decisions voicing my concerns, making new friends from different parts of the world, adapting to living with a roommate, and ultimately choosing the correct major I need in order to obtain the career path I find best for myself. All the changes were scary at first, but I had Big Kitty with me to help provide stability in this new experience in my life.

annotations.text: All the changes were scary at first, but I had Big Kitty with me to help provide stability in this new experience in my life.

paragraphs.body: I can say that I have been through a lot of hardships and changes in my life that should have affected the person I am today. But there was one object that helped me along the way and made me the person I am today. Over the years, Big Kitty’s appearance has become worn down representing all the times I needed her to provide me with the stability I needed. So anytime I feel the least bit out of control or shortchanges happen, I look for Big Kitty and hug her tight.
